title: Interleukin-2-inducible t-cell kinase inhibitors to produce enhanced immune responses
abstract: Provided are, inter alia , vaccines comprising an interleukin-2-inducible T-cell kinase (ITK) inhibitor and an antigen, methods of producing an enhanced immune response in a subject by administering the vaccine to the subject, and methods of producing an enhanced immune response in a subject by administering to the subject an effective amount of an ITK inhibitor and an effective amount of an antigen. In embodiments, the ITK inhibitor is a compound of formula (I) or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof, wherein the substituents are as defined herein.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method of producing an immune response in a patient in need thereof, the method comprising administering to the patient: (a) an effective amount of an interleukin-2-inducible T-cell kinase inhibitor, and an effective amount of an antigen; or (b) an effective amount of an interleukin-2-inducible T-cell kinase inhibitor, and a vaccine comprising an effective amount of an antigen and a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier; thereby producing the immune response in the patient. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the interleukin-2-inducible T-cell kinase inhibitor is soquelitinib or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the antigen is a protein, a nucleic acid, a polysaccharide, or a glycoconjugate. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the antigen is a cancer antigen, a viral antigen, a bacterial antigen, a fungal antigen, or a parasitic antigen. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the antigen is a cancer antigen; wherein the cancer antigen is alpha-fetoprotein, carcinoembryonic antigen, trophoblast glycoprotein precursor, immature laminin receptor protein, fetal sulphoglycoprotein, g-fetoprotein, cc2-H- ferroportin, beta-2-microglobulin, beta-human chorionic gonadotropin, calcitonin, bladder tumor antigen, CD117, CA15-3, CA19-9, CA-125, CA 27.29, CA72-4, CD20, CD22, CD25, CD30, CD33, chromogranin A, cytokeratin fragment 21-1, estrogen receptor, progesterone receptor, fibrinogen, gastrin, HE4, Her2/neu, neuron-specific enolase, nuclear matrix protein 22, prostatic acid phosphatase, PD-L1, PD-1, prostate-specific antigen, somatostatin receptor, thyroglobulin, 5-HIAA, osteocalcin, transferrin recepto, alkaline phosphtase, BRAF, KRAS, NMP22, BRCA2, urokinase plasminogen activator, apoliprotein A1, S100, nestin, cytokeratin fragments 21-1, ferritin, tissue polypeptide antigen, epididymal secretory protein E4, CECAM 5, CECAM 6, serum M-protein, CTLA-4, B7-1/B7-2, MUC-1, epithelial tumor antigen, tyrosinase, melanoma- associated antigen, p53, mutated p53, MART-2, beta-catenin, mutated ras, mutated KRAS, BAGE, GAGE-1, NY-ESO-1, PRAME, CT83, SSX2, ART-4, BAGE, beta-catenin/m, Bcr-abL CAMEL, CAP-1 , CASP-8, CDC27/m, CD 4/m, a cell surface protein of the claudin family, c- MYC, CT, Cyp-B, DAM, ELF2M, ETV6-AML1, G250, GAGE, GnT-V, KPL-1, HAGE, HPV- 

E7, HPV-E6, HAST-2, hTERT, LAGE, LDLR/FUT, MAGE- A, MAGE-B, MAGE-C, MART- 1, Melan-A, MCIR-1, myosin, MUM-1, MUM-2, MUM-3, NA88-A, NF1, NY-ESO-1, NY-BR- 1, HSP90, BCR-ABL-1, RAR, PRAME, proteinase 3, PSM, RAGE, RU1, RU2, SAGE, SART- 1, SART-3, SCGB3A2, SCP 1, SCP2, SCP3, SSX, Survrvin, TEL/AML1, TPEM, TRP-1, TRP- 2, TRP-2-INT2, TPTE, WT, or a combination of two or more thereof. 6. The method of claim 4, wherein the antigen is a viral antigen; wherein the viral antigen is a component of the virus, a protein from the virus, or a carbohydrate from the virus. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the virus is cytomegalovirus, Epstein-Barr virus, human immunodeficiency virus, monkeypox, Ebola, West Nile virus, Zika virus, chikungunya virus, dengue virus, coronavirus, hepatitis A, hepatitis B, herpes zoster, human papillomavirus, measles, mumps, poliomyelitis, rabies, respiratory syncytial virus, rotavirus, rubella, seasonal influenza, smallpox, or varicella. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein the antigen is an antigen to anthrax, cholera, diphtheria, Haemophilus influenzae type b, meningococcal disease, pertussis, pneumococcal disease, tetanus, tuberculosis, malaria, or typhoid fever. 9. The method of claim 1, comprising administering to the patient about 250 mg to about 1,000 mg of the interleukin-2-inducible T-cell kinase inhibitor per day for a period of time; or administering to the patient about 300 mg to about 500 mg of the interleukin-2- inducible T-cell kinase inhibitor per day for a period of time; or administering to the patient about 400 mg of the interleukin-2-inducible T-cell kinase inhibitor per day per day for a period of time. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein the period of time is from about 1 day to about 28 days, or from about 1 day to about 21 days, or from about 1 day to about 14 days, or from about 1 day to about 7 days. 11. The method of claim 1, wherein the interleukin-2-inducible T-cell kinase inhibitor is a compound of Formula (I) or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof, wherein the compound of Formula (I) is:
